fundamental_analysis.details: Weak ROE of 9.2%; High debt to equity ratio of 1.1; Operating margin data not available; Current ratio data not available

consistency_analysis.details: Inconsistent earnings growth pattern; Total earnings growth of -15.7% over past 10 periods

book_value_analysis.details: Consistent book value per share growth (Buffett's favorite metric); Book value CAGR: 2.4%

management_analysis.details: Company has been repurchasing shares (shareholder-friendly); No significant new stock issuance detected; No or minimal dividends paid

macro_context.market_sentiment: Broadly positive into April month-end — S&P 500 +0.46% to 7,168, Dow +1.49% to 49,588, Nasdaq +0.21%. April is tracking for best month since late 2020, driven by strong US GDP data and AI-fueled business investment. Big Tech earnings mixed (Meta -9%, MSFT -5%, GOOGL +9%) but market absorbing results constructively.
macro_context.fed_outlook: Fed held rates at 3.5%-3.75% on April 29 (third consecutive hold). Unusual 8-4 dissent split signals deep internal disagreement. Inflation still elevated partly due to global energy prices; easing bias retained in statement. Powell effectively stepping down as chair — leadership transition adds policy uncertainty.
macro_context.geopolitical: Middle East conflict (Strait of Hormuz closure risk) driving elevated oil prices and inflation uncertainty. U.S.-Venezuela tensions and NATO-Greenland posture add uncertainty. Tariff environment under Trump remains a wildcard. IMF cut global growth forecast to 3.1% for 2026 with headline inflation at 4.4%.
key_events: Fed rate decision April 29 — held at 3.5%-3.75%, dovish statement retained but split 8-4
Big Tech Q1 earnings wave wrapping up (Meta, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN reported late April)
Middle East conflict — Strait of Hormuz risk keeping energy prices elevated
Powell leadership transition at the Fed — new chair expected to be named soon
US midterm elections looming as political risk factor for rest of 2026

ticker_news.headline: Bank of America Q1 2026 EPS $1.11 beats estimate $1.01 by 9.9%; raises full-year NII growth guidance to 6%-8%
BAC completes $14 billion bond sale to finance Oracle's major new data center in Michigan
BAC and Alaska Air Group extend multi-year co-branded credit card agreement, expanding Atmos Rewards portfolio
Bank of America redeems $3 billion in 3.559% Fixed/Floating Rate Senior Notes ahead of April 2027 maturity
Daiwa Securities raises BAC price target from $58 to $61 following strong Q1 results

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: Post-Q1 earnings (April 16): Piper Sandler raised PT $53->$59 (neutral); Truist raised PT $57->$61 (buy); Daiwa raised PT $58->$61 (Apr 28). UBS lowered PT $67->$62 but kept buy (Apr 7). March: Erste Group downgraded from strong-buy to hold. January: Wolfe Research downgraded from outperform to peer perform. Morningstar raised fair value to $65 from $58. Overall: 40% strong buy, 45% buy, 15% hold, 0% sell (20 analysts); median PT $62, range $53-$71, current price ~$47.23 implying ~28% upside to consensus.

web_context.competitor_activity: JPMorgan Chase market cap (~$800B) now exceeds combined value of BAC ($344B) + Citi ($168B) + WFC ($260B). Morningstar considers BAC the 'best idea' among major banks at current valuations. Wells Fargo is the sector laggard (down 8% YTD) due to NII guidance miss — making BAC's raised NII guidance comparatively more impressive. Citi is relative outperformer on low valuation and Treasury/trade momentum. BAC and WFC identified as primary beneficiaries of yield curve normalization in 2026.
web_context.earnings_info: Q1 2026 reported April 15, 2026: EPS $1.11 (beat est $1.01 by 9.9%), NII $15.745B, net income $8.584B, share repurchases $10.849B (213.6M shares). Full-year 2026 NII growth guidance raised to 6%-8%. Next earnings: Q2 2026 expected July 14, 2026; consensus revenue estimate ~$29.87B.
